body_txt: This trope describes names and other terms which are not spelled consistently in officially published materials (and not fan-made translations), usually because of transliteration issues. This most commonly happens in translations of Japanese media including Anime & Manga , although it also crops up in other languages. Situations include anything from vowel additions to unique-cipher dropping due to phoneme sets and writing systems. English, for example, is famous for its many ways and rules of spelling (e.g., Americans dropping extra vowels such as in the word color , as compared with its Commonwealth spelling colour ). Japanese has separate vowel-heavy syllabic and ideographic writing systems. Spanish has several familiar-looking letter combinations intended to be pronounced in specific ways. Complicating the issue is that some names simply become popular enough in other languages that they're modified to fit them better, and you can't be sure if it's actually intended to be meaningful . In other cases there may be a specific foreign name that the original writer clearly intended given context, but which the translator was apparently not aware of and so erroneously interpreted. Related to Adaptation Dye-Job (and/or other Adaptation Tropes , as the case may be). Compare with Rouge Angles of Satin , when a word or name is spelled incorrectly in a way that it is confused with another existent word, and Viewer Name Confusion , for when the name is spelled consistently but the viewers get it wrong. noreallife
